target.name: Apple
target.notes_excerpt: Consumer technology company deploying AI via Apple Intelligence across its device ecosystem. Avoids public regulatory advocacy but leverages privacy standards as a competitive differentiator.

evidence: Title: CEO of OpenAI, Chairman of Helion Energy, President of Y Combinator (until 2019)

source.name: Sam Altman
source.notes_excerpt: Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI, the artificial intelligence research organization that developed ChatGPT. He became CEO of OpenAI in 2019 after co-founding the organization in 2015. In November 2023, he was briefly fired by OpenAI's board of directors but was reinstated five days later. His net worth was estimated at US$3.3 billion by Forbes in March 2026.

target.name: Helion Energy
target.notes_excerpt: Private nuclear fusion company building the Orion plant in Chelan County, WA, targeting power delivery to Microsoft data centers by 2028 under the world's first fusion power purchase agreement. Backed by Sam Altman ($350M+) and SoftBank; raised $425M Series F in Jan 2025 at a $5.4B valuation. Directly tied to AI compute energy demands.

He clerked for Judge Richard Posner on the 7th Circuit Court of Appeals and Justice Antonin Scalia on the United States Supreme Court. target.name: Stanford Law School
target.notes_excerpt: Home of CodeX (Stanford Center for Legal Informatics) and the Stanford Law AI Initiative co-directed by Prof. Nathaniel Persily. Hosts AI governance courses, the Law Policy Lab practicum on federal AI use in criminal justice, and applied research projects such as ComplianceTwin (2025–2026).

evidence: Jennifer Pahlka is a senior fellow at the Niskanen Center and the Federation of American Scientists

source.name: Jennifer Pahlka
source.notes_excerpt: Jennifer Pahlka is the founder of Code for America and author of 'Recoding America: Why Government Is Failing in the Digital Age and How We Can Do Better'. She served as U.S. Deputy Chief Technology Officer under President Obama from 2013-2014, where she helped found the United States Digital Service. She is currently a senior fellow at the Niskanen Center and the Federation of American Scientists

target.name: Niskanen Center
target.notes_excerpt: Centrist/center-right think tank founded 2015 by Jerry Taylor (ex-Cato). Named "Most Interesting Think Tank" by TIME (2023). AI policy stance: regulatory restraint, intervene only when harm is realistic. Has 501(c)(3) research arm and 501(c)(4) lobbying arm. Major funders include Hewlett Foundation, Open Philanthropy, Carnegie Corp.

target.name: Federation of American Scientists
target.notes_excerpt: DC-based science and technology policy nonprofit founded in 1945. FAS runs the Day One Project to translate scientific expertise into federal policy; active in AI governance, advocating for responsible AI innovation, NIST AISI funding, and federal AI adoption. Submitted AI Action Plan recommendations to the White House in 2025.
